title: A Surprise Birthday Picnic at Target
description: Follow Sam and Aaron as they shop at Target to prepare a surprise birthday picnic for their friend Mina.

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to English Listening Channel.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到英文聽力頻道。

sentences.englishOriginal: Today, we're going to look at a conversation at a Target store.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 今天，我們要來看看一段在 Target 百貨公司的對話。

vocabularyNotes.title: conversation
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「對話」或「交談」，通常指兩人或多人之間非正式的意見與想法交流。
examples: I had a long conversation with my boss today.
Their conversation was filled with laughter.

grammarNotes.title: be going to + verb (future plan)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「be going to」後面接原形動詞，用來表示事先已經計畫好、或即將發生的未來動作。
examples: We are going to visit Kyoto next month.
Look at those clouds! It is going to rain.

sentences.englishOriginal: In this video, you will follow a simple and practical story between two friends, Sam and Aaron, and some store staff.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在這段影片中，你將跟隨兩位朋友 Sam 和 Aaron，以及幾位店員之間一個簡單又實用的故事。

vocabularyNotes.title: practical
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「實用的」或「切合實際的」，指某事物能應用於日常生活中。
examples: He gave me some practical advice on budgeting.
Learning a new language is very practical for traveling.

vocabularyNotes.title: staff
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指全體「全體職員」或「員工」，是集合名詞，通常後面接單數或複數動詞皆可，但在這裡指商店裡的工作人員。
examples: The hotel staff were incredibly helpful during our stay.
We need to hire more sales staff for the holidays.

sentences.englishOriginal: Don't worry, I will speak slowly and clearly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 別擔心，我會講得很慢且很清晰。

phraseNotes.title: Don't worry
phraseNotes.explanation: 日常生活中極為常見的固定片語，意思是「別擔心」、「沒關係」，用來安慰或寬慰對方。
examples: Don't worry about the mess; I can clean it up later.
Don't worry, everything will be just fine.

sentences.englishOriginal: So, please relax and enjoy the story.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 所以，請放鬆心情，好好享受這個故事吧。

sentences.englishOriginal: Before we start, I have a small question for you.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在我們開始之前，我有一個小問題要問你。

sentences.englishOriginal: Please listen carefully to find the answer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 請仔細聽，來找出答案。

sentences.englishOriginal: This will help you understand English more deeply.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這會幫助你更深層地理解英文。

sentences.englishOriginal: So, here is today's question: What are Sam and Aaron shopping for?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那麼，今天的問題是：Sam 和 Aaron 正在採購什麼東西？

phraseNotes.title: shop for
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指「採購、購買特定物品或為特定目的購物」。後面通常接想購買的商品或活動。
examples: I'm shopping for a new winter coat today.
They spent the afternoon shopping for groceries.

sentences.englishOriginal: The answer is mentioned in the conversation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 答案會在對話中提到。

vocabularyNotes.title: mention
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「提及」、「談到」。這裡使用的是被動語態（is mentioned），表示答案被提及。
examples: Did he mention when the meeting would start?
As I mentioned before, the store closes early on Sundays.

sentences.englishOriginal: If you find the answer, please write it in the comments section.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果你找到了答案，請把它寫在評論區。

sentences.englishOriginal: By writing your answer in English, you can improve your English skills even more effectively.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 藉由用英文寫下你的答案，你能更有效地提升你的英文能力。

grammarNotes.title: By + V-ing (method/means)
grammarNotes.explanation: 介係詞「by」後面接動名詞（V-ing），用來表示「透過某種方式、手段或方法」來達成後續的結果。
examples: By practicing every day, she became a great pianist.
You can save power by turning off the lights when you leave.

sentences.englishOriginal: All right, let's start the video. Are you ready? Okay, let's begin.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好的，我們開始看影片吧。準備好了嗎？好，我們開始囉。

sentences.englishOriginal: Here we are. Look at that big red Target on the wall.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們到囉。看看牆上那個巨大的紅色 Target 標誌。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Here we are
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 母語者在到達某個目的地時常用的口語，意思是「我們到了」或「這就是我們要找的地方」。
examples: Here we are! This is the restaurant I told you about.
Pull over here. Okay, here we are.

sentences.englishOriginal: I know. It is so easy to see from the road.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我知道，從路上看過去真的很顯眼。

sentences.englishOriginal: Their mascot is a dog, right?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們的吉祥物是一隻狗，對吧？

vocabularyNotes.title: mascot
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「吉祥物」，通常代表某個球隊、品牌、學校或企業。
examples: The team's mascot is a friendly green dragon.
Many Japanese cities have their own unique mascot.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, it is a white dog named Bullseye. It has a red mark on one eye.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯，是一隻名叫 Bullseye（靶心）的白狗。牠的一隻眼睛周圍有一個紅色標記。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is a cute name. Okay, let me grab a cart.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那個名字真可愛。好啦，我去推一台推車。

vocabularyNotes.title: cart
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 在超市或量販店指的是「購物手推車」（shopping cart 的簡稱）。
examples: Please put the heavy items at the bottom of the cart.
The wheel of this shopping cart is broken.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: grab a cart
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 動詞「grab」字面意思是抓取，但在生活口語中，母語者常用它來代替「get」或「take」，表示「順手拿一個」、「順便推一台推車」，聽起來非常自然、生活化。
examples: Let's grab a coffee before the movie starts.
Can you grab a shopping basket for me?

sentences.englishOriginal: Get a red one. They are right by the door.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 推一台紅色的。推車就在門旁邊。

phraseNotes.title: right by
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「就在...正旁邊」，其中的「right」用作加強語氣，強調距離非常近。
examples: My office is right by the elevator.
She was sitting right by the window.

sentences.englishOriginal: Got it. Now, what are we shopping for today?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 收到。那我們今天到底要買什麼？

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Got it
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常道地的口語，意思是「我知道了」、「明白了」、「收到」，多用於答應對方的要求或表示理解對方的話。
examples: A: Make sure to lock the back door. B: Got it!
I finally got it after he explained it twice.

sentences.englishOriginal: We are shopping for Mina's birthday, remember? It is a surprise.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們要幫 Mina 慶祝生日買東西啊，記得嗎？這是一個驚喜。

vocabularyNotes.title: surprise
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「驚喜」或「意想不到的事物」，在這裡指給 Mina 瞞著她準備的驚喜派對。
examples: The party was a complete surprise to her.
I have a little surprise for you in the kitchen.

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, right. The birthday picnic. We need everything for it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，對喔。生日野餐。我們需要準備野餐的所有東西。

vocabularyNotes.title: picnic
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「野餐」，通常指帶食物到戶外公園或郊外享用。
examples: Let's have a picnic in the botanical garden this weekend.
We packed sandwiches and fresh fruit for our picnic.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, a picnic in the park just for her. Here is my list on my phone.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯，在公園裡專門為她辦的野餐。我的清單在手機裡，給你看。

sentences.englishOriginal: Let me see. Food, drinks, paper plates, cups, and a blanket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我看看。食物、飲料、紙盤、杯子，還有一張毯子。

vocabularyNotes.title: blanket
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「毯子」或「毛毯」。在野餐的語境中，指鋪在草地上的野餐墊／野餐毯（picnic blanket）。
examples: It gets a bit cold at night, so you might need an extra blanket.
We spread out a large blanket on the grass.

phraseNotes.title: Let me see
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用的口語短語，意思是「讓我看看」、「讓我想想」，常在思考、查看某物或點算清單時下意識脫口而出。
examples: Let me see... where did I leave my keys?
Let me see your new haircut!

sentences.englishOriginal: The blanket is the big one. We need a nice yellow picnic blanket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 毯子是大件物品。我們需要一張漂亮的金黃色野餐毯。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yellow? Why yellow?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 黃色？為什麼是黃色？

sentences.englishOriginal: Because yellow is Mina's favorite color. This is the one thing we must find.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 因為黃色是 Mina 最喜歡的顏色。這是我們絕對必須找到的一樣東西。

sentences.englishOriginal: Okay, the yellow blanket is very important. I will remember.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好，黃色毯子非常重要。我會記住的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Good. The store is really big. Let's not stay here all day.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 很好。這家店真的超大。我們別在這裡耗一整天。

phraseNotes.title: all day
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「一整天」或「整日」。
examples: He played video games all day yesterday.
I've been working on this report all day.

sentences.englishOriginal: I agree. Should we split up and go two ways?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我同意。我們要不要分頭行動，分兩路走？

phraseNotes.title: split up
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意思是「分頭」、「分開行動」。在購物或逛街時，常用來指大家分開去找各自需要的東西。
examples: Let's split up to find the ingredients, then meet at the checkout.
The group split up after visiting the museum entrance.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, that is smart. You take the food and the drinks.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對，那樣很聰明。你負責拿食物和飲料。

sentences.englishOriginal: And you take the blanket and the paper plates?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那你去拿毯子和紙盤囉？

sentences.englishOriginal: Perfect. I will text you when I am done.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 完美。我弄好之後會傳簡訊給你。

grammarNotes.title: Time clauses with Present Tense
grammarNotes.explanation: 在由 when, after, before 等引導的時間副詞子句中，即使指的是未來發生的動作，子句中也要用現在式（I am done）來代替未來式。
examples: I will call you when I arrive at the station.
Please turn off the AC before you leave.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: text someone
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 在現代英語中，「text」常直接當作動詞使用，意思是「傳簡訊給某人」，比講「send a text message to someone」更簡潔地道。
examples: Text me when you get home safely.
I texted her this morning, but she hasn't replied yet.

sentences.englishOriginal: Sounds good. Let's meet in the middle later.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 聽起來不錯。待會我們在中間會合。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Sounds good
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中省略了主詞「That」，用來表示贊同對方的提議或計畫，意思是「好啊」、「聽起來不錯」。
examples: A: Let's grab sushi tonight. B: Sounds good!
Sounds good to me, see you at seven.

sentences.englishOriginal: Great plan. Oh, and please get some fruit, too.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好計畫。噢，還有，也請順便買點水果。

sentences.englishOriginal: Fruit for the picnic. I can do that.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 野餐用的水果。這交給我沒問題。

sentences.englishOriginal: Thank you. This is going to be so much fun for Mina.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 謝謝。這對 Mina 來說一定會非常好玩。

sentences.englishOriginal: She will love it. She has been so busy at work lately.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她一定會很喜歡的。她最近工作真的太忙了。

grammarNotes.title: Present Perfect Tense with 'lately'
grammarNotes.explanation: 「lately」（最近）這個副詞通常與現在完成式（has been）連用，用來描述從過去一段時間持續到現在的狀態或習慣。
examples: Have you seen any good movies lately?
He has been feeling a bit tired lately.

sentences.englishOriginal: I know. She really needs a happy, relaxing day.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我知道。她真的很需要一個快樂、放鬆的一天。

vocabularyNotes.title: relaxing
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「令人放鬆的」，用來修飾事物、活動或日子（例如一個令人放鬆的假期或一天）。
examples: A warm bath is so relaxing after a long run.
We had a very relaxing weekend at the beach.

sentences.englishOriginal: A picnic in the park is perfect for that.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在公園野餐最適合不過了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, warm sun, good food, and all her friends.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯，溫暖的陽光、美食，還有她所有的朋友。

sentences.englishOriginal: And a big yellow blanket to sit on.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 還有一張可以坐在上面的大黃色毯子。

sentences.englishOriginal: [laughter] You will not forget the blanket now.
sentences.chineseTranslation: ［笑聲］你現在絕對不會忘記這張毯子了。

sentences.englishOriginal: No way. Yellow blanket, the first thing on my list.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 絕不可能。黃色毯子，我清單上的第一要務。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: No way
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常口語且強烈的否定表達，意思是「不可能」、「絕不」、「沒門」，這裡用來強調自己絕對不會忘記。
examples: A: Can I borrow your car? B: No way, you don't even have a license!
No way! I can't believe we won the lottery!

sentences.englishOriginal: Good. Okay, see you soon.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 很好。好啦，待會見。

sentences.englishOriginal: Excuse me, can you help me? I am a little lost.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不好意思，可以幫我一下嗎？我有點迷路了。

phraseNotes.title: a little lost
phraseNotes.explanation: 「lost」是迷路，加上「a little」修飾，表示「稍微、有點迷失方向或找不到地方」。
examples: I'm a little lost inside this airport terminal.
If you get a little lost, just use your map app.

sentences.englishOriginal: Of course. That is my job. What are you looking for?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當然可以。這是我的工作。您正在找什麼呢？

phraseNotes.title: look for
phraseNotes.explanation: 常見片語，意思是「尋找」某人或某物。
examples: What are you looking for under the desk?
I am looking for a gift for my sister.

sentences.englishOriginal: I knew you worked here. I saw the red shirt.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我知道您在這裡工作。我看到那件紅襯衫了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, all the team wear a red shirt. That is how you find us.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯，我們所有的團隊員工都穿紅襯衫。這就是您辨識我們的方式。

grammarNotes.title: Collective noun 'team' with plural verb
grammarNotes.explanation: 在這裡「team」被視為複數概念（指團隊裡的各個成員），因此後面搭配了複數動詞「wear」而非 wears。這在英式及部分美式口語中很常見。
examples: The team are celebrating their victory tonight.
Our support team wear uniform polo shirts.

sentences.englishOriginal: That is helpful. The red shirt is easy to see in a big store.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那很有幫助。在大商店裡，紅襯衫真的很醒目、很好認。

sentences.englishOriginal: Right. So, what do you need today?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對啊。那麼，您今天需要買點什麼？

sentences.englishOriginal: I need drinks. Where are the drinks, please?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我需要買飲料。請問飲料區在哪裡？

sentences.englishOriginal: The drinks are in aisle six on the left side.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 飲料在左手邊的第六排走道。

vocabularyNotes.title: aisle
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，發音為 /aɪl/（其中的字母 s 不發音）。指商店、超市、教堂或飛機座位之間的「走道」或「貨架通道」。
examples: You can find the cereal in aisle three.
An aisle seat is preferred by many frequent flyers.

sentences.englishOriginal: Aisle six. Thank you. I need water and juice.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 第六走道。謝謝您。我需要水和果汁。

sentences.englishOriginal: We have both. There is a big shelf of them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們兩種都有。那裡有一大個貨架都是。

vocabularyNotes.title: shelf
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「貨架」或「層架」。其複數形為 shelves。
examples: The top shelf is too high for me to reach.
Can you put these books back on the shelf?

sentences.englishOriginal: Great. I'm shopping for a birthday picnic.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 太棒了。我正在採購生日野餐要用的東西。

sentences.englishOriginal: How nice. A picnic is always fun in summer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 真好。夏天辦野餐總是特別好玩。

sentences.englishOriginal: It is for my friend Mina. It is a surprise for her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 是為了我的朋友 Mina 辦的。這是給她的驚喜。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is so sweet. Do you need anything else?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那真是太貼心了。您還需要其他東西嗎？

phraseNotes.title: anything else
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「其他任何東西」，通常用於問句或否定句中，店員很常用這句話來詢問顧客是否還有其他需求。
examples: Would you like anything else with your coffee?
Is there anything else I can do for you?

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, I need some fresh fruit. Where can I find it?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對，我還需要一些新鮮水果。請問在哪裡可以找到？

sentences.englishOriginal: The fresh fruit is over there in produce.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 新鮮水果在在那邊的生鮮蔬果區（produce）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Produce? What is produce?
sentences.chineseTranslation: Produce？什麼是 produce 呀？

sentences.englishOriginal: Produce is the fresh fruit and vegetables. It is all in one place.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「Produce」指的就是新鮮的水果和蔬菜。它們都集中在同一個地方。

vocabularyNotes.title: produce
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 注意這裡作名詞用，發音重音在第一個音節 /ˈproʊduːs/。意思是「（尤指集體栽種的）農產品、生鮮蔬果」，為不可數名詞。如果是動詞「生產」，重音則在後面 /prəˈduːs/。
examples: We buy our fresh produce at the local farmers' market.
The supermarket sells both organic and conventional produce.

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, I see. So, apples and oranges are in produce.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，我懂了。所以蘋果和柳丁都算在生鮮蔬果區內。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Oh, I see
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為道地的口語，意思是「噢，我明白了」或「原來是這樣」，「see」在這裡代表理解、明白。
examples: A: The library is closed on Mondays. B: Oh, I see. Thanks.
Oh, I see what you mean now.

sentences.englishOriginal: Exactly. We have strawberries, too. They are very good this week.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 完全正確。我們還有草莓。這週的草莓品質非常好喔。

vocabularyNotes.title: exactly
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞，意思是「沒錯」、「完全正確」，常用於口語中強烈贊同或肯定對方說的話。
examples: A: So we need to turn left here? B: Exactly!
I know exactly how you feel.

sentences.englishOriginal: Strawberries are perfect for a picnic. I will get some.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 草莓最適合野餐了。我拿一些。

sentences.englishOriginal: Good choice. The produce section is next to the drinks.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好選擇。蔬果區就在飲料區隔壁。

phraseNotes.title: next to
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「在...旁邊」、「緊鄰著...」。
examples: The bakery is right next to the bank.
He sat next to me during the flight.

sentences.englishOriginal: So, I can get everything in one walk. That is easy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這樣一來，我走一趟就能把東西買齊了。真簡單。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, take your time and look around.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 是的，慢慢來，到處逛逛看看。

phraseNotes.title: take your time
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用片語，意思是「慢慢來」、「不急」，用來告訴對方不需要匆忙。
examples: Take your time filling out the form; there's no rush.
You can take your time to make a decision.

phraseNotes.title: look around
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「環顧四周」或「隨意逛逛、參觀」。
examples: We have some time before the train, let's look around the shop.
Feel free to look around and ask if you have questions.

sentences.englishOriginal: I will. One more question, if that is okay.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我會的。如果可以的話，想再請教一個問題。

phraseNotes.title: One more
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「再一個」、「多一個」。
examples: Can I have one more cup of tea, please?
Just one more kilometer and we'll be there.

sentences.englishOriginal: Of course, ask me anything.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當然可以，儘管問。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: ask me anything
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常大方熱情的口語表達，意思是「你想問什麼都可以」、「儘管問我任何問題」。
examples: I'm here to help you study, so ask me anything.
Feel free to ask me anything about the project.

sentences.englishOriginal: Do you have small bags of chips for a picnic snack?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 請問你們有賣適合野餐當點心的小包裝洋芋片嗎？

vocabularyNotes.title: chips
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 在美式英語中指「洋芋片、洋芋脆片」（potato chips 的簡稱）。在英式英語中 chips 通常指炸薯條。
examples: He bought a bag of barbecue-flavored chips.
Pass me the bowl of chips, please.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, we do. They are in aisle four, near the front.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有的。它們在第四排走道，靠近前方的位置。

sentences.englishOriginal: Perfect. A picnic needs some chips and snacks.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 太棒了。野餐不可缺少洋芋片和零食。

sentences.englishOriginal: I agree. Get the big bag with many small bags inside.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我同意。去拿那種裡面有很多小包裝的大組合包。

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, that is smart. Then everyone gets their own.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，那樣很聰明耶。這樣每個人都能拿到屬於自己的一包。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: gets their own
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 意思是「每個人拿到自己專屬的一份」。「their」在這裡當作單數中性代名詞，用來代替前面代表單數大眾的「everyone」。
examples: At the party, everyone gets their own cupcake.
Make sure each student gets their own textbook.

sentences.englishOriginal: Exactly. It's easy and there is no mess.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯。這很方便，而且不會弄得亂七八糟。

vocabularyNotes.title: mess
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「混亂」、「髒亂」。「there is no mess」指的是不會製造髒亂或清理起來很省事。
examples: The children made a huge mess in the living room.
I hate cooking because it always creates a mess.

sentences.englishOriginal: Great tip. Thank you so much for your help.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 很實用的建議。非常謝謝您的幫忙。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Great tip
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中常用「tip」表示「實用的建議、秘訣、小撇步」。「Great tip」意思就是「真是不錯的建議／妙招」。
examples: Thanks for the cleaning tip; it worked perfectly!
Here's a useful tip for traveling on a budget.

sentences.englishOriginal: You're welcome. I hope Mina has a wonderful day.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不客氣。希望 Mina 度過美好的一天。

phraseNotes.title: You're welcome
phraseNotes.explanation: 最標準且常見的「不客氣」，用來回應對方的道謝。
examples: A: Thanks for holding the door. B: You're welcome!

sentences.englishOriginal: I am sure she will. Thanks again. Have a great day. Enjoy the picnic.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我相信她一定會的。再次感謝您。祝您有美好的一天。祝野餐愉快。

sentences.englishOriginal: Excuse me, I am looking for a picnic blanket, but I cannot find it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不好意思，我正在找野餐毯，但我找不到。

sentences.englishOriginal: A picnic blanket? Let me help you. Come this way.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 野餐毯嗎？我來幫您。請往這邊走。

phraseNotes.title: come this way
phraseNotes.explanation: 帶路時常說的片語，意思是「往這邊走」、「請跟我來」。
examples: Your table is ready, please come this way.
Follow me, come this way to the exit.

sentences.englishOriginal: Thank you. I looked everywhere and saw nothing.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 謝謝您。我各個地方都找過了，什麼也沒看到。

sentences.englishOriginal: The blankets are not with the other bedding. They're in the seasonal section.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這些毯子沒有跟其他床上用品放在一起。它們被擺在季節性商品區（seasonal section）。

vocabularyNotes.title: bedding
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「床上用品」，例如被套、床單、枕頭套、被子等統稱。
examples: We bought some new cotton bedding for the guest room.
The bedding department is on the second floor.

sentences.englishOriginal: The seasonal section? What is that?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 季節性商品區？那是什麼區呀？

vocabularyNotes.title: seasonal
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「季節性的」或「隨季節變化的」，衍生自名詞 season（季節）。
examples: The restaurant menu changes regularly to offer seasonal food.
Pumpkin spice latte is a popular seasonal drink in autumn.

sentences.englishOriginal: It is a part of the store that changes with the time of year.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 它是商店內的一個區域，會隨著一年之中的不同時節而更換擺設的商品。

phraseNotes.title: with the time of year
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「隨著一年的特定時間或時節變化」。
examples: Hotel prices always fluctuate with the time of year.
The weather varies greatly with the time of year in this region.

grammarNotes.title: Relative clause with 'that'
grammarNotes.explanation: 「that changes with the time of year」是一個關係子句（形容詞子句），用來修飾前面的先行詞「a part of the store」，that 在子句中擔任主詞。
examples: This is a book that belongs to my sister.
I want to buy a computer that runs very fast.

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, so it is different in summer and winter?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，所以它在夏天和冬天賣的東西會不一樣囉？

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes. In summer, this section is all picnic and beach things.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 是的。在夏天，這個區域全都是賣野餐和海灘用品。

sentences.englishOriginal: So, that is why the blankets are here now.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 原來如此，這就是為什麼毯子現在會擺在這裡的原因。

phraseNotes.title: that is why
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「那就是為什麼...」或「這就是原因」，用來連接前因與後果。
examples: He stayed up late last night; that is why he looks so tired today.
I lost my keys, and that is why I'm late.

sentences.englishOriginal: Right. In winter, we put warm coats and gloves here instead.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯。冬天時，我們反而會在這裡擺放保暖外套和手套。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is smart. The store changes with the seasons.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那很聰明。商店會隨季節做調整。

sentences.englishOriginal: We do. Now, here are the picnic blankets.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們確實如此。好啦，這幾款就是野餐毯了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, no. Most of them are gone. The shelf is almost empty.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，糟了。大部分都被買光了。貨架幾乎是空的。

sentences.englishOriginal: It is a busy weekend. Let me look in the back.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這週末生意很好。我去後方的倉庫幫您找找看。

phraseNotes.title: in the back
phraseNotes.explanation: 在零售商店的語境下，這句話通常指「在後方的倉庫」或「員工儲藏室」。
examples: We don't have this size on display, let me check in the back.
The manager is in the back counting the cash.

sentences.englishOriginal: I really need a yellow one. It's for my friend's birthday.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我真的很需要一條黃色的。是要送給我朋友的生日禮物。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yellow? Wait a second. I think I see one right here.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 黃色嗎？稍等一下。我想我看到這裡好像有一條。

phraseNotes.title: wait a second
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語常見短語，意思是「等一下」、「稍等一秒鐘」，通常在突然發現某物或想事情時使用。
examples: Wait a second, let me double-check this number.
Wait a second! I think I left my umbrella inside.

sentences.englishOriginal: Where? I do not see it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 哪裡呀？我沒看到耶。

sentences.englishOriginal: Look on the bottom shelf behind this box.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 看最底層的貨架，就在這個箱子後面。

vocabularyNotes.title: bottom
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞或形容詞，意思是「底部」或「最下方的」。「bottom shelf」即是指最底層的架子。
examples: The signature is located at the bottom of the page.
He found his keys on the bottom rack of the shoe cabinet.

sentences.englishOriginal: You found it! A yellow blanket and it's the last one.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 您找到了！一條黃色毯子，而且是最後一條！

phraseNotes.title: the last one
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「最後一個」或「最後一件商品」。
examples: Hurry up! This cookie is the last one in the box.
I managed to buy the last one on the shelf.

sentences.englishOriginal: You are very lucky. This is the only yellow one left.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 您非常幸運。這是唯一剩下的一條黃色毯子了。

grammarNotes.title: Past participle as adjective (left)
grammarNotes.explanation: 動詞 leave 的過去分詞「left」常直接放在名詞或代名詞（這裡的 yellow one）後面，用來表示「剩下的、留下來的」。
examples: Is there any pizza left from last night?
There are only five minutes left before the exam ends.

sentences.englishOriginal: This is perfect. My friend Mina loves yellow.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這太完美了。我的朋友 Mina 最喜歡黃色。

sentences.englishOriginal: It is a nice big blanket. Many people can sit on it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這是一條又大又好看的毯子。很多人都可以坐在上面。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is great. We will have four or five people at the picnic.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那太棒了。我們的野餐大概會有四到五個人參加。

sentences.englishOriginal: Then this size is just right for you.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那這個尺寸對您來說剛好合適。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: just right
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 意思是「恰到好處」、「剛剛好」，用來形容大小、溫度、分量或情況非常合適完美。
examples: The soup is not too hot, it's just right.
This medium size shirt fits me just right.

sentences.englishOriginal: Can I ask you one more thing while I'm here?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 既然我都到這了，可以再順便請教您一件事嗎？

grammarNotes.title: Conjunction 'while' for simultaneous actions
grammarNotes.explanation: 「while」引導時間副詞子句，表示「當...的同時」或「趁著某個狀態期間」，此處指趁著人還在商店此區域的期間順便詢問。
examples: Please watch the baby while I cook dinner.
While you are out, could you buy some milk?

nativeExpressionNotes.title: while I'm here
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 日常生活常用表達，意思是「既然我都在這裡了（就順便...）」，是一種很有效率的口語銜接詞。
examples: While I'm here at the bank, I should update my passbook.
Can I grab my coat from your room while I'm here?

sentences.englishOriginal: Sure, go ahead.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當然可以，請說／請便。

phraseNotes.title: go ahead
phraseNotes.explanation: 非常道地的口語片語，意思是「請繼續」、「請做吧」、「請說」，用來允許或鼓勵對方採取行動。
examples: A: Can I use this pen? B: Sure, go ahead.
If you are ready to give your speech, go ahead.

sentences.englishOriginal: Do you have small folding chairs for older friends?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 請問你們有賣給年長朋友坐的小型折疊椅嗎？

vocabularyNotes.title: folding
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「可折疊的」，通常用來形容桌子、椅子、腳踏車等家具或工具。
examples: We brought a few folding tables for the outdoor market.
This folding umbrella fits perfectly in my bag.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, they are right at the end of this section.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有的，它們就在這個區域的最末端。

phraseNotes.title: at the end of
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「在...的盡頭」或「在...的末端／結尾」。
examples: The restroom is at the end of the long hallway.
We will review the project details at the end of the meeting.

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, good. Some people do not like to sit on the ground.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，太好了。有些人不太喜歡直接坐在地上。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is true. A chair is easier for them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 確實如此。有椅子對他們來說比較輕鬆、方便。

sentences.englishOriginal: I will get two chairs then, just to be safe.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那我買兩張椅子好了，保險起見。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: just to be safe
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 日常超實用口語，意思是「保險起見」、「以防萬一」，用來解釋自己採取某個預防性動作的原因。
examples: Take an umbrella with you, just to be safe.
I locked the drawer twice, just to be safe.

sentences.englishOriginal: Good idea. They are light and easy to carry.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好主意。它們很輕，而且很好攜帶。

grammarNotes.title: Adjective + infinitive structure (easy to carry)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「easy to carry」是一種「形容詞 + 不定詞（to + V）」的結構，用來表示主詞（椅子）在某個動作上具有什麼特質（很容易攜帶）。
examples: This glass cup is very fragile and hard to wash.
English is not easy to learn, but it is interesting.

sentences.englishOriginal: Thank you so much. You helped me a lot.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 非常謝謝您。您真的幫了我大忙。

sentences.englishOriginal: My pleasure. I'm happy I could help.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不客氣，這是我的榮幸。很高興能幫上忙。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: My pleasure
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常道地且優雅的客套話，常用來回應對方的道謝，比一般的 You're welcome 更具熱情和禮貌，意思是「這是我的榮幸／我很樂意」。
examples: A: Thanks for driving me home. B: My pleasure!

sentences.englishOriginal: Have a good day. Thank you again. You too. Enjoy the birthday picnic.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 祝您有美好的一天。再次感謝。您也是，祝你們生日野餐玩得開心。

sentences.englishOriginal: Aaron, over here! I got your text.
sentences.chineseTranslation: Aaron，在這邊！我收到你的簡訊了。

sentences.englishOriginal: There you are. Look at my cart. I have big news.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你來啦。看我的推車。我有大消息要告訴你。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: There you are
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 當你一直在等某人，或者終於在人群中看到對方時，會脫口而出的話，意思是「你來啦」、「你人在這呀」。
examples: There you are! I've been looking for you everywhere.
Ah, there you are. Can you help me with this box?

sentences.englishOriginal: You found it, the yellow blanket. It is beautiful.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你找到了，那張黃色毯子。它好漂亮喔。

sentences.englishOriginal: It was the last one. A team member helped me find it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這可是最後一條呢。有一位店員（團隊成員）幫我找到的。

phraseNotes.title: team member
phraseNotes.explanation: 字面意思是「團隊成員」，在 Target 百貨公司內，這也是他們對店內「正職員工／店員」的正式稱呼方式。
examples: If you need an item from the stockroom, please ask a team member.
She has been a dedicated team member for five years.

sentences.englishOriginal: That is so lucky. Mina is going to love that color.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那真的太幸運了。Mina 一定會超喜歡這個顏色。

sentences.englishOriginal: I know. And look, I got the paper plates and cups, too.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我知道。而且你看，我也拿了紙盤和紙杯了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Nice! I got the drinks, the snacks, and some strawberries.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 讚啦！我拿了飲料、零食，還有一些草莓。

sentences.englishOriginal: Strawberries, great idea. They're perfect for a picnic.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 草莓，好主意。它們超適合野餐的。

phraseNotes.title: great idea
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「好主意」、「真棒的想法」，用來讚賞別人的提議。
examples: A: Let's order pizza for dinner. B: Great idea!

sentences.englishOriginal: We got food, plates, and a blanket, all in one store.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們在同一家店裡就買齊了食物、盤子和毯子。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is why I like big stores. One trip and we are done.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這就是為什麼我喜歡大型量販店。跑一趟就搞定了。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: One trip and we are done
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 很口語且生活化的句型，意思是「出門一趟就把事情辦完／解決」，強調高效率、免去舟車勞頓。
examples: If we plan carefully, we can do all our errands in one trip and be done.

sentences.englishOriginal: It is true. We do not need to drive to five places.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 真的。我們不需要大老遠開車跑五個不同的地方。

sentences.englishOriginal: Right. Food, home things, clothes, and everything is here.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯。食物、家居用品、衣服，所有東西這裡通通都有。

sentences.englishOriginal: One-stop shopping. It saves so much time.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一站式購物。這省下了超多時間。

vocabularyNotes.title: one-stop
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「一站式的」、「全方位的」，指在同一個地方就能滿足所有相關需求。
examples: This portal provides a one-stop service for new residents.
Walmart is a famous one-stop supermarket in the US.

sentences.englishOriginal: Let's add a few more things while we are in home goods.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 趁我們還在家居用品區（home goods），再來多挑幾樣東西吧。

phraseNotes.title: home goods
phraseNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「家居用品」，包括床單、裝飾品、廚房配件等生活居家雜貨。
examples: I love browsing the home goods section for new scented candles.
They spend thousands of dollars on home goods for their new apartment.

sentences.englishOriginal: Good idea. We need napkins for the picnic.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好主意。我們需要野餐用的餐巾紙。

vocabularyNotes.title: napkin
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「餐巾」或「餐巾紙」，通常在用餐時用來擦嘴或擦手。
examples: Please pass me a paper napkin; I spilled some sauce.
Cloth napkins look more elegant on a formal dinner table.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, get a pack of napkins. People always need them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對，拿一包餐巾紙。大家總是會用到。

phraseNotes.title: a pack of
phraseNotes.explanation: 量詞片語，意思是「一包...」或「一捆...」，常用於包裝在一起的日常用品。
examples: He bought a pack of gum at the convenience store.
We need another pack of batteries for the remote control.

sentences.englishOriginal: Done. What else is on the list?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 拿好囉。清單上還有什麼別的東西嗎？

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Done
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中直接單用「Done」，意思是「辦好了」、「搞定了」、「完成了」，此處指Aaron把餐巾紙放進購物車後，向對方回報這一項已經解決。
examples: A: Make sure to email the client. B: Done!
Is your homework finished? Yes, done.

sentences.englishOriginal: Let me check my phone. Plates, cups, napkins. Wait.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我查一下手機。盤子、杯子、餐巾紙。等等。

sentences.englishOriginal: What is wrong? Did we forget something?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 怎麼了？我們漏掉什麼東西了嗎？

phraseNotes.title: What is wrong?
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「怎麼了？」、「出什麼事了？」，用來關切神色不對或話說到一半突然停住的朋友。
examples: What is wrong? You look really pale.
My computer suddenly stopped working. I don't know what is wrong.

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, no. We forgot the candles for the birthday cake.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，天啊。我們忘記拿生日蛋糕用的蠟燭了！

vocabularyNotes.title: candle
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「蠟燭」。在這裡特指生日蠟燭（birthday candles）。
examples: We blew out the candles on the cake after making wishes.
The room was lit by a single scented candle.

sentences.englishOriginal: The candles! You are right. We cannot sing without candles.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 蠟燭！你說得對。沒有蠟燭我們就沒辦法唱生日快樂歌了。

sentences.englishOriginal: I do not see them here in home goods. Where are they?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我在這個家居用品區沒看到它們。它們到底擺在哪裡啊？

sentences.englishOriginal: I'm not sure. Let's keep looking as we walk.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我也不太確定。我們邊走邊繼續找吧。

grammarNotes.title: Keep + V-ing (continuous action)
grammarNotes.explanation: 動詞「keep」後面固定接動名詞（V-ing），用來表示「持續、一直做某個動作」而不停歇。
examples: Don't give up; just keep trying your best.
She kept talking for an hour without any break.

sentences.englishOriginal: Okay, we will find them somewhere in the store.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好啦，我們一定會在店裡的某個角落找到它們的。

sentences.englishOriginal: We have to. A birthday needs candles.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一定要找到啊。過生日必備蠟燭耶。

sentences.englishOriginal: Don't worry. This store has everything, remember?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 別擔心。這家店可是什麼都有賣，記得嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: True. Let's keep shopping and watch for them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 也是。我們繼續逛，並且一路上多留意一下。

phraseNotes.title: watch for
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「密切注意、留意...的出現」，通常用來指尋找某個特定的物品或等待某事的發生。
examples: Watch for the street sign on your right side.
We need to watch for any changes in the patient's condition.

sentences.englishOriginal: Good plan. Onward.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好計畫。前進吧！

vocabularyNotes.title: onward
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞或形容詞，意思是「向前地」、「前進地」。在口語中單用可以帶有「繼續出發、繼續向前走」的幽默或精神抖擻的語氣。
examples: The troops marched onward through the heavy mud.
Let's focus on our goals from this point onward.

sentences.englishOriginal: Look, we are walking past the electronic section now.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 看，我們現在正路過電子產品區（electronic section）。

phraseNotes.title: walk past
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「走路經過」或「路過」某個地方。
examples: I walk past this coffee shop every single morning on my way to work.
She walked past me without saying a word.

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, right. Phones, TVs, and all the tech things.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 噢，對。手機、電視，還有各式各樣的科技產品。

sentences.englishOriginal: Do we need anything from here for the picnic?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們有需要從這一區買什麼野餐要用的東西嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: Hmm, let me think. We want lots of photos, right?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 嗯...讓我想想。我們到時候想要拍超多照片，對吧？

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, many photos of Mina in the whole day.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯，紀錄 Mina 一整天的許多照片。

sentences.englishOriginal: Do our phones have enough battery for photos? My phone runs out of battery fast.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那我們的個人手機電量夠用來拍照嗎？我的手機耗電非常快耶。

vocabularyNotes.title: battery
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「電池」或「手機電量」。
examples: My laptop battery is low, so I need a charger.
These toys require three AA batteries to operate.

phraseNotes.title: run out of
phraseNotes.explanation: 非常實用的動詞片語，意思是「用光」、「耗盡」某樣物資或資源（例如時間、金錢、電力等）。
examples: Hurry up! We are running out of time.
The car ran out of gas on the highway.

sentences.englishOriginal: That is a good point. My phone is old, too.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這點說得很好（你提醒得有道理）。我的手機也很舊了。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: That is a good point
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常實用的口語認同語，意思是「你說的很有道理」或「這點提得很對」，當對方指出一個你之前沒想到的細節或論點時很好用。
examples: A: We should leave early to avoid the rush hour traffic. B: That is a good point.
I hadn't considered the cost factor, that's a good point.

sentences.englishOriginal: We do not want to miss the best moments.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們可不想錯過那些最美好的精采瞬間。

vocabularyNotes.title: moment
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「瞬間」、「時刻」或「片刻」。
examples: It was a truly proud moment for the entire family.
Please wait a moment; the doctor will see you shortly.

sentences.englishOriginal: Let's grab a charger, just in case.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那我們順便拿一個充電器吧，以防萬一。

vocabularyNotes.title: charger
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「充電器」。
examples: Can I borrow your phone charger for a few minutes?
I forgot to pack my wireless charger for the trip.

phraseNotes.title: just in case
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用固定短語，意思是「以防萬一」，通常加在句尾，說明做某事是為了應付未預料到的突發狀況。
examples: I'll take an extra set of keys with me, just in case.
It might rain later, so keep your jacket handy just in case.

sentences.englishOriginal: Just in case is smart. A dead phone is the worst.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「以防萬一」很聰明。手機沒電真的很慘（是最糟糕的情況）。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: a dead phone
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 「dead」在形容電子產品時，指的是「沒電了、壞掉了、完全無法開機的狀態」，所以母語者講 a dead phone 就是指完全沒電的手機，非常生動地道。
examples: I couldn't call a taxi because I was stuck with a dead phone.
My battery is at 1%, it's going to be dead in a second.

sentences.englishOriginal: Here is a phone charger. It is a good price.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這裡有一個手機充電器。價格挺划算的。

phraseNotes.title: a good price
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「划算的價格」或「合理的價錢」，表示這件物品不貴、物超所值。
examples: We bought this couch at a really good price during the anniversary sale.
It's hard to find a good apartment at a good price nowadays.

sentences.englishOriginal: Perfect. Now we can take photos all day long.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 完美。現在我們可以整天拍個夠了。

phraseNotes.title: take photos
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「拍照」。也可以說 take pictures。
examples: She likes to take photos of her food before eating.
They stood in front of the castle to take a photo together.

phraseNotes.title: all day long
phraseNotes.explanation: 是 all day 的加強版，語氣更強調「整整一整天、從早到晚都...」。
examples: It rained all day long, so we stayed indoors.
The radio played the same song all day long.

sentences.englishOriginal: I want a photo of Mina when she sees the yellow blanket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當 Mina 看到那張黃色毯子的那一刻，我一定要幫她拍張照。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, her happy face is the best photo of the day.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯，她快樂的表情絕對會是當天最棒的一張照片。

sentences.englishOriginal: And a photo of everyone eating the strawberries.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 還有大家一起吃草莓的照片。

sentences.englishOriginal: So many photos. We really do need the charger.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有這麼多照片要拍。我們確實真的很需要這個充電器。

grammarNotes.title: Emphatic 'do' with verbs
grammarNotes.explanation: 在肯定句中，可以在一般動詞（need）前面加上助動詞「do/does/did」來表示「特別強調、確實、真的」，用來加強整句話的語氣。
examples: I do believe that he is telling the truth.
She does look beautiful in that white gown.

sentences.englishOriginal: And we can charge in the car on the way home.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且我們在回家的路上還可以直接在車裡充電。

vocabularyNotes.title: charge
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「充電」。
examples: Where can I charge my phone in this library?
It takes about two hours to charge the device fully.

phraseNotes.title: on the way home
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「在回家的路上」。如果是在去某處的路上，可以說 on the way to ... 。
examples: I picked up some dinner on the way home from work.
We can stop by the post office on the way home.

sentences.englishOriginal: Great thinking. Let me check our budget quickly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 心思真縝密。讓我快速核對一下我們的預算。

vocabularyNotes.title: budget
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「預算」，指為特定活動或目的規劃的可用總金額。
examples: We need to stay within our budget for this vacation.
The marketing department has a limited budget this year.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Great thinking
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 意思是「想得太周到了」或「好主意／聰明的思考方式」，常用來誇獎對方考慮事情很周全或提出一個很有建設性的看法。
examples: A: Let's bring some bug spray just in case. B: Great thinking!

sentences.englishOriginal: Sure. How much have we spent so far?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好啊。目前為止我們已經花了多少錢？

phraseNotes.title: so far
phraseNotes.explanation: 常見片語，意思是「到目前為止」，常常和現在完成式（have spent）搭配使用。
examples: So far, everything is going according to the plan.
We have received twenty applications so far.

sentences.englishOriginal: Let me add it up: food, drinks, plates, cups, blanket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 讓我加總一下：食物、飲料、盤子、杯子、毯子。

phraseNotes.title: add up
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意思是「把...加起來」或「計算總和」。
examples: If you add up all these bills, it comes to a large sum.
The accountant is adding up the expenses from last month.

sentences.englishOriginal: And now napkins and a charger.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 現在還要加上餐巾紙和一個充電器。

sentences.englishOriginal: We're still okay. We have some money left.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們金額還挺充裕的。還剩下一點錢。

sentences.englishOriginal: Good. We did not spend too much.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 很好。我們沒有花過頭。

phraseNotes.title: too much
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「太多」或「過度」，這裡修飾花費的金額。
examples: Don't drink too much coffee before bed.
I think I spent too much money this weekend.

sentences.englishOriginal: No, we are careful shoppers. That's nice.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對啊，我們是精打細算的消費者。那挺好的。

sentences.englishOriginal: It is easy to buy too much in a big store.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在大超市裡真的很一不小心就會買太多東西。

grammarNotes.title: It is + adjective + to + verb structure
grammarNotes.explanation: 虛主詞虛主詞「It」的結構。其中的「It」沒有實質語意，真正的主詞是後面的不定詞片語「to buy too much in a big store」。
examples: It is important to brush your teeth twice a day.
It is difficult to finish this task in one hour.

sentences.englishOriginal: So true. There are fun things everywhere.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 千真萬確。到處都是有趣的小玩意。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: So true
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中表示「完全同意、真的是這樣」的極簡說法，用來附和對方的觀點。
examples: A: Adult life is so full of stress. B: So true.

sentences.englishOriginal: But we have our list and we're almost done.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不過好在我們有帶清單，而且我們也差不多快買齊了。

phraseNotes.title: almost done
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「快要完成了」、「差不多要結束了」。
examples: Give me five minutes, I'm almost done with my laundry.
The building construction is almost done.

sentences.englishOriginal: Just the candles left. We still haven't found them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 就只剩下蠟燭了。我們到現在都還沒找到它們。

sentences.englishOriginal: Right. Let's not forget. I keep thinking about the candles.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對。可別忘了。我腦袋裡一直惦記著蠟燭這件事。

phraseNotes.title: think about
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「思考、考慮」或「心中惦記著」某事。
examples: What are you thinking about so deeply?
I need some time to think about your proposal.

sentences.englishOriginal: Keep looking. They must be near the front.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 繼續找吧。它們一定擺在靠近門口（前排）的地方。

phraseNotes.title: near the front
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「靠近前面／靠近結帳櫃檯或出口」的區域。
examples: The premium seats are located near the front of the theater.
You can drop off your cart near the front entrance.

sentences.englishOriginal: Maybe. Let's head to the checkout now.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有可能。我們先往結帳櫃檯（checkout）走吧。

vocabularyNotes.title: checkout
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指超級市場或商店的「結帳櫃檯」或「收銀台」。
examples: The line at the checkout was extremely long.
Please pay for your items at the front checkout.

phraseNotes.title: head to
phraseNotes.explanation: 非常道地的動詞片語，意思是「前往」、「朝著某個方向出發」。
examples: After class, we are going to head to the night market.
It's getting late; let's head to the train station.

sentences.englishOriginal: Good idea. We can look on the way.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好主意。我們可以順路沿途找找看。

phraseNotes.title: on the way
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「在途中的路上、順路」。
examples: Can you drop this letter in the mailbox? It's on the way.
I'll explain everything to you on the way.

sentences.englishOriginal: Okay, here is the self-checkout. Let's pay and go.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好啦，這邊就是自助結帳櫃檯（self-checkout）。我們付錢走人吧。

vocabularyNotes.title: self-checkout
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「自助結帳櫃檯」，由顧客自己掃描條碼並付款的機器系統，在歐美大型量販店及現代超市非常普及。
examples: Using the self-checkout is usually faster if you only have a few items.
I don't know how to scan this vegetable at the self-checkout.

sentences.englishOriginal: Good. I will scan and you can bag the things.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好。我來負責掃描條碼，你負責把東西裝袋。

vocabularyNotes.title: bag
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 注意這裡用作動詞！意思是「把物品裝進袋子裡」。日常去超市時非常實用。
examples: The cashier asked if I wanted him to bag the milk separately.
Please help me bag these groceries while I pay.

sentences.englishOriginal: Sounds good. Pass me the strawberries first.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒問題。先幫我把草莓遞過來。

vocabularyNotes.title: pass
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「傳遞」、「拿給」，常接雙受詞（pass me the strawberries 表示把草莓拿給我）。
examples: Could you pass me the salt, please?
He passed the ball to his teammate just in time.

sentences.englishOriginal: Here. Beep. One box of strawberries. Now the drinks, water and juice. Beep. Beep. The machine is fast today.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 給。［嗶］一盒草莓。接下來是飲料，水和果汁。［嗶］［嗶］。這台機器今天反應挺快的嘛。

sentences.englishOriginal: Next, the paper plates and cups. Beep. And the napkins. Beep. We are almost done. Just the blanket left.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 下一個，紙盤和紙杯。［嗶］。還有餐巾紙。［嗶］。我們快弄好囉。只剩毯子了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Here it is. The yellow blanket. Beep. It is in the bag now.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在這邊。黃色毯子。［嗶］。現在已經裝進袋子裡囉。

sentences.englishOriginal: Great. But wait, we never found the candles.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 太好了。等等，我們自始至終都沒找到蠟燭耶。

sentences.englishOriginal: I know. I feel bad. A birthday needs candles.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我知道。我覺得有點過意不去。過生日是一定要有蠟燭的。

phraseNotes.title: feel bad
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「感到內疚、過意不去」或「心情不好／遺憾」。
examples: I feel bad for missing your graduation ceremony.
Don't feel bad about making a mistake; everyone does.

sentences.englishOriginal: Hey, look at this little shelf right here by the register.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 嘿，你看收銀機正旁邊的這個小置物架。

vocabularyNotes.title: register
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「收銀機」（cash register 的簡稱）。
examples: Please leave your items on the counter next to the register.
The cashier opened the register to give me change.

sentences.englishOriginal: What do you see? Gum, batteries, and candles. Birthday candles!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你看見了什麼？口香糖、電池，還有蠟燭。是生日蠟燭！

vocabularyNotes.title: gum
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「口香糖」（chewing gum 的簡稱）。
examples: Do you have a piece of mint gum?
Chewing gum is not allowed during the exam.

sentences.englishOriginal: No way! The candles were by the register the whole time.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 誇張耶！蠟燭居然自始至終都擺在收銀台旁邊。

phraseNotes.title: the whole time
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「自始至終」、「一直、全程」，用來強調某個狀態在整個過程中都沒有改變過。
examples: I was wearing my glasses the whole time and didn't realize it.
She sat silently in the corner during the whole time.

sentences.englishOriginal: We walked past them at the end. We looked all over the store, and they were right here.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們最後走過來的時候路過了它們。我們找遍了整家店，結果它們就近在眼前。

phraseNotes.title: look all over
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「找遍各個地方」或「到處尋找」。
examples: I've looked all over the house, but I still can't find my passport.
They searched all over the park for the lost dog.

sentences.englishOriginal: This is the small things shelf. Stores put small things here.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這是小雜物架。商店都會把小東西擺在這一區。

sentences.englishOriginal: Why do they put them right by the register?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們為什麼要把這些東西故意擺在收銀台正旁邊呀？

sentences.englishOriginal: You see them while you wait. You buy them without a plan.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你在排隊等待的時候就會看到它們。這樣你就會毫無計畫地（衝動）買下來。

phraseNotes.title: without a plan
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「沒有事先計畫地」，常用來形容行銷學上的「衝動購物」（impulse buying）。
examples: Never go grocery shopping when you are hungry, or you will buy lots of things without a plan.

sentences.englishOriginal: Huh, that is smart. Gum, batteries, candles, all small things.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 哈，那招真聰明。口香糖、電池、蠟燭，全都是小東西。

sentences.englishOriginal: Right. You see them and think, 'Oh, I need that.'
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒錯。你一看到就會心想：『噢，我剛好需要這個。』

sentences.englishOriginal: Well, this time we really do need them. Beep.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好吧，不過我們這次是真的很需要它。［嗶］。

sentences.englishOriginal: Perfect. Now we have the candles, too. The last thing on the list. We are all done.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 完美。現在我們也有蠟燭了。清單上的最後一項。我們全部大功告成囉。

sentences.englishOriginal: Let me pay. Tap the card here.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我來付錢吧。把卡片在這邊感應一下。

vocabularyNotes.title: tap
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，原意是「輕觸、輕拍」。在現代購物付款的語境下，「tap the card」意思是「用信用卡或電子錢包進行感應式付款」。
examples: You can just tap your phone on the terminal to pay.
Please tap your transit card here when you board the bus.

sentences.englishOriginal: Great. And here's our receipt.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 太棒了。這張是我們的發票／收據。

vocabularyNotes.title: receipt
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，發音為 /rɪˈsiːt/（其中的字母 p 不發音）。意思是「收據」或「發票」。
examples: Always keep your receipt in case you need to return the item.
Can I get a printed receipt, please?

sentences.englishOriginal: We did it! Every single thing on the list.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們成功啦！買齊了清單上的每一件東西。

phraseNotes.title: Every single thing
phraseNotes.explanation: 「every thing」的加強版，其中的「single」用來強調「完完全全、每一件都沒有漏掉」。
examples: I remembered every single thing she told me.
He packed every single thing he owned into one suitcase.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: We did it
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 當順利完成了一項任務或達成目標時，用來歡呼、慶祝的道地口語，意思是「我們做到了！」或「我們成功了！」。
examples: We did it! We finally finished the group project.
After months of training, she did it—she ran the full marathon.

sentences.englishOriginal: Mina's picnic is ready. Let's go.
sentences.chineseTranslation: Mina 的野餐物資準備就緒。我們走吧。

sentences.englishOriginal: Wait. Before we go, let's sit at the cafe.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 等等。在我們走之前，先去店裡的附設咖啡廳坐坐吧。

sentences.englishOriginal: Oh, they have a cafe right inside the store? Yes. Look, it is right by the door. We can rest here.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 哇，他們商店裡面居然就有附設咖啡廳？對啊。你看，就在大門旁邊而已。我們可以在這裡休息一下。

sentences.englishOriginal: That is nice. My feet are a little tired. Mine, too. Let's sit and check our bags.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那挺好的。我的腳有點酸了。我的也是。我們坐下來核對一下袋子裡的東西吧。

sentences.englishOriginal: Good idea. I want to make sure we have everything.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好主意。我想確認一下我們是不是真的都有買齊。

phraseNotes.title: make sure
phraseNotes.explanation: 極常見的片語，意思是「確保」、「確認」某事無誤。
examples: Please make sure you turn off the stove before leaving.
I just want to double-check and make sure the reservation is correct.

sentences.englishOriginal: Okay, let me look in the bags one more time. We have the food, the drinks, and the strawberries. Check.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好，讓我再看一眼袋子。我們有食物、飲料和草莓。［確認。］

phraseNotes.title: one more time
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「再一次」，相當於 once again。
examples: Can you sing that song one more time?
Let's practice the dance routine one more time.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Check
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 在逐項清點、核對清單物品時，口語上講「Check!」就相當於中文在表格打勾時說「有了！」、「確認！」或「過關！」。
examples: A: Passport? B: Check. A: Tickets? B: Check.

sentences.englishOriginal: And the paper plates, cups, and napkins. Check. The charger for our photos. Check.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 還有紙盤、紙杯和餐巾紙。［確認。］用來拍照片的充電器。［確認。］

sentences.englishOriginal: And of course, the birthday candles. The candles we almost forgot. Check.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當然，還有生日蠟燭。那盒我們差點就漏掉的蠟燭。［確認。］

sentences.englishOriginal: And here, at the bottom, the most important thing: the yellow blanket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後這裡，在最底下，最重要的一樣東西：黃色毯子。

sentences.englishOriginal: Everything for Mina's picnic and the yellow blanket. It is perfect. She is going to be so happy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這就是 Mina 野餐的所有物資，還有那張黃色毯子。太完美了。她一定會超級開心。

sentences.englishOriginal: I can see it now: all of us on the yellow blanket, strawberries, drinks, and a birthday cake in the park with candles on the cake and Mina smiling.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我現在已經能想像出那個畫面了：我們大家一起坐在黃色毯子上，在公園裡吃著草莓、喝著飲料、切著生日蛋糕，蛋糕上點著蠟燭，而 Mina 臉上掛著幸福的笑容。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: I can see it now
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 意思是「我現在完全能夠想像出那個畫面了」，通常用於對某個美好的未來場景充滿期待並展開生動想像時。
examples: We are going to have a beautiful beach house. Ah, I can see it now.

sentences.englishOriginal: It will be a wonderful surprise. She has no idea.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這一定會是一個超棒的驚喜。她現在完全不知情呢。

phraseNotes.title: have no idea
phraseNotes.explanation: 非常常用的片語，意思是「完全不知道」或「毫無頭緒」，相當於 completely don't know。在這裡指 Mina 對這場偷偷籌辦的生日派對毫無察覺。
examples: I have no idea where I left my glasses.
She had no idea that we were planning a party for her.

sentences.englishOriginal: None at all. This is going to be the best day.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 完全不知道。這天一定會成為最棒的一天。

phraseNotes.title: none at all
phraseNotes.explanation: 用來強烈否定，意思是「一點也沒有」、「完全沒有」，這裡附和前一句，強調她真的一丁點概念或察覺都沒有。
examples: A: Do you have any money left? B: None at all.
I feel no pain now, none at all.

sentences.englishOriginal: We did great today. One store and we got everything.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們今天表現得太棒了。只逛一家店就買齊了所有東西。

sentences.englishOriginal: One-stop shopping, remember? It really saved us time.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一站式購物嘛，還記得嗎？這真的幫我們省了不少時間。

sentences.englishOriginal: It did. Now let's finish our drinks and head out.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 確實如此。那我們趕快把飲料喝完，然後出發吧。

phraseNotes.title: head out
phraseNotes.explanation: 生活口語片語，意思是「出發」、「出門」或「離開某地前往他處」。
examples: It's already eight o'clock; we should head out now.
What time are we heading out tomorrow morning?

sentences.englishOriginal: Sounds good. The park is only 10 minutes away.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 聽起來不錯。公園距離這裡只要 10 分鐘車程（路程）。

phraseNotes.title: minutes away
phraseNotes.explanation: 用來表示時間上的距離，意思是「距離...只有幾分鐘的路程／時間」。
examples: My school is just five minutes away from my house.
The train station is ten minutes away if you walk fast.

sentences.englishOriginal: Then let's set up the picnic before Mina arrives.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那我們就在 Mina 抵達之前，先把野餐場地布置好。

vocabularyNotes.title: arrive
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「抵達」、「到達」。
examples: The train is scheduled to arrive at noon.
We arrived at the hotel safely after a long flight.

phraseNotes.title: set up
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意思是「布置」、「準備」或「架設」某個活動現場、設備或攤位。
examples: The crew spent hours setting up the stage for the concert.
Let's set up the tent before it gets completely dark.

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, grab the bags. The yellow blanket goes first.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好，提起袋子。黃色毯子先打頭陣（最先拿出來鋪）。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: goes first
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 意思是「最優先、第一順位進行」，在這裡指鋪野餐墊是野餐布置的第一步驟。
examples: When packing a suitcase, heavy items always go first.
Safety always goes first in this factory.

sentences.englishOriginal: [laughter] Of course it does. After you. Here we go. Happy birthday, Mina!
sentences.chineseTranslation: ［笑聲］那當然囉。您先請。我們走吧。祝妳生日快樂，Mina！

nativeExpressionNotes.title: After you
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極具紳士風度、非常道地的禮貌用語，當你要禮讓對方先走、先進門或先採取行動時使用，意思是「您先請」。
examples: A: Oh, are we both entering the elevator? B: After you!

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Here we go
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 常用口語，用在「行動正式開始、出發」的一刻，意思是「我們出發囉！」或「要開始囉！」。
examples: Are you ready for the roller coaster? Okay, here we go!

sentences.englishOriginal: How was it? I hope this video helped you improve your English.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 覺得如何呢？希望這段影片能對你提升英文能力有所幫助。

sentences.englishOriginal: Now, let me ask the question from the beginning one more time: What are Sam and Aaron shopping for?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 現在，讓我再次提問影片開頭的那個問題：Sam 和 Aaron 正在採購什麼東西？

sentences.englishOriginal: The answer was mentioned in the video. Did you find it?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 答案剛才已經在影片中提到了。你找到了嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: If you did, please write your answer in the comments section.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果你找到了，請在評論區寫下你的答案。

sentences.englishOriginal: By practicing English output like this, you can improve your English skills more efficiently.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 藉由進行像這樣的英文輸出練習，你可以更有效率地提升你的英文技能。

vocabularyNotes.title: output
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，意思是「輸出」。在語言學習中，聽與讀是輸入（input），而說與寫則是輸出（output）。
examples: To speak fluently, you need to increase your daily English output.
The system error logs all the input and output data.

sentences.englishOriginal: On this channel, I share stories in slow and easy English with small but useful tips to help you learn.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在這個頻道上，我用慢速且簡單的英文分享故事，並提供微小但實用的小技巧來協助你學習。

sentences.englishOriginal: I will keep uploading videos like this, so if you enjoyed it, please subscribe to the channel.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我會持續上傳像這樣的影片，所以如果你喜歡這部影片，請訂閱本頻道。

vocabularyNotes.title: subscribe
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「訂閱」，常用於網路影音平台、電子報或雜誌，固定與介係詞 to 連用（subscribe to）。
examples: Don't forget to click the bell icon after you subscribe to our channel.
I subscribe to a financial magazine to stay updated on economic trends.

sentences.englishOriginal: And please don't forget to like the video and leave a comment.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 也請別忘了給這部影片點個讚，並留下你的評論。

phraseNotes.title: leave a comment
phraseNotes.explanation: 社群網路常用語，意思是「留言」或「留下評論」。
examples: If you have questions about the recipe, just leave a comment below.

sentences.englishOriginal: I also offer channel memberships. If you enjoyed this video, please consider joining as a member.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我也有提供頻道會員福利。如果你喜歡這部影片，請考慮加入成為會員。

vocabularyNotes.title: consider
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「考慮」或「斟酌」，後面若加動詞必須接動名詞 V-ing（此處接的是 joining）。
examples: We are considering moving to a bigger apartment next year.
Please take your time to consider our offer.

phraseNotes.title: channel membership
phraseNotes.explanation: 名詞，指網路影音平台（如 YouTube）的「頻道會員資格」。
examples: Channel membership perks include exclusive badges and loyalty emojis.

sentences.englishOriginal: All right, that's all for today. I hope to see you in the next video. See you next time. Bye.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好啦，今天就到這裡。希望能在下一部影片見到你。下次見囉，掰掰。

phraseNotes.title: that's all for today
phraseNotes.explanation: 非常道地的生活及課堂結束用語，意思是「今天就到此為止」、「今天就這樣囉」。
examples: We have covered all the major topics, so that's all for today. Class dismissed.
